summary: Justin Brennan analyzes an off-market bank-owned 44-unit deal in McAllen, Texas priced at $2.7M with 37% down payment ($220K). He walks through underwriting methodology including expense ratio benchmarking, rent growth assumptions, renovation cost per unit ($5-6K), refinance scenarios, and investor capital account tracking, demonstrating how to verify income/expenses and structure returns.
takeaways: Target 18% or greater return on cost (ROC) for renovation metrics to justify capex spend
Use 5-year fixed loans on stable value-add deals to enable refinancing flexibility without prepayment penalties, enabling rate drops 3+ years out
Benchmark expense ratios: 35-45% for 1980-2010 properties, targeting mid-40s for 1993-era assets; Texas properties run higher due to insurance/taxes
Conduct lease audits verifying names match between leases and rent roll; require bank statements if seller cannot produce T12 financials with supporting docs
Establish three separate checking accounts within property LLC: operating (property manager access only), capital improvement, and entity/investor account (owner-controlled)
Structure equity splits (70/30 GP-LP preferred over 80/20) and 6% preferred returns (not 8%) to optimize investor distributions
Never allow property manager to establish or control bank accounts; this creates leverage risk if terminating management

skill_candidates.change: Implement expense ratio benchmarking by property vintage (35-45% range for 1980-2010 builds) rather than accepting proforma assumptions uncritically
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin corrected the McAllen deal underwriting by adjusting management/insurance expenses from stated 54%+ down to mid-40s using industry benchmarks, which materially improved returns and hit return thresholds

skill_candidates.change: Require full lease audits and bank statement verification before accepting T12 financials from sellers lacking systematic accounting
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin's exchange with Doug on the PSA deal emphasized: 'money's going in and coming out of bank accounts...if you don't have the reporting I need to verify...show me the bank accounts. Period.' This prevents overstating occupancy/rents.

skill_candidates.change: Conduct in-person market trips to convert cold broker relationships into pre-market deal flow, prioritizing face-to-face credibility over phone/email outreach
skill_candidates.evidence: Chutna's breakthrough: after in-person trip to Cincinnati, brokers began sharing pre-market deals because 'face to face made them take me more seriously' and see her as serious operator, not hobbyist

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - How to Analyze Off-Market Deals_05/12/26 | Justin Brennan
